blog.title: Your Guide to Education and Life

residencyPermitContent.title: Student Residence Permit
residencyPermitContent.description: A mandatory residence permit type for foreign students who will pursue associate, undergraduate, graduate, or doctoral education in Turkey.
residencyPermitContent.importantInfoText1: Student residence permit application is made through the
residencyPermitContent.importantInfoText2: system. After filling out the application form, you may need to submit it along with the required documents to your university's International Student Office (or the unit designated by the Immigration Office) within
residencyPermitContent.warningText2: for open education or distance learning programs. You must physically attend education in Turkey.

requiredDocuments.title: Application Form
requiredDocuments.desc: Wet-signed form obtained from the e-Residence system.

requiredDocuments.title: Passport
requiredDocuments.desc: Original and photocopies of processed pages.

requiredDocuments.title: Student Certificate
requiredDocuments.desc: Current wet-signed or e-signed document showing active student status.

requiredDocuments.title: Biometric Photo
requiredDocuments.desc: 4 photos with white background taken within the last 6 months.

requiredDocuments.title: Health Insurance
requiredDocuments.desc: A valid private health insurance policy in Turkey.

requiredDocuments.title: Address Document
requiredDocuments.desc: Notarized rental contract or dormitory document.

requiredDocuments.title: Card Fee Receipt
requiredDocuments.desc: Tax office receipt proving payment of the valuable paper fee.

requiredDocuments.title: UETS Document
requiredDocuments.desc: Notification address document obtained via e-Government or PTT.

residencyPermitContent.processChangeDepartmentDesc: Notification must be made within 20 days for faculty/department changes within the same city. If transferring to a different city, a new application must be submitted to that city's Immigration Office within 10 days.
residencyPermitContent.processFreezeRegistrationDesc: Residence permits of students who freeze their registration for any reason are canceled. When returning to education, a new initial application must be made.
residencyPermitContent.faq1Question: What happens to my residence permit after graduation?
residencyPermitContent.faq1Answer: Your student residence permit ends as of your graduation date. If you plan to stay in Turkey, you must switch to a 'Short-Term Residence Permit' within 10 days (application can be made within 6 months after graduation).
residencyPermitContent.faq2Question: Can I both study and work?
residencyPermitContent.faq2Answer: Student residence permit does not directly grant work rights. Associate and undergraduate students can obtain part-time work permits after their first year. Graduate and doctoral students can obtain work permits without time restrictions.
residencyPermitContent.faq3Question: Do I need to pay a fee?
residencyPermitContent.faq3Answer: Foreigners are exempt from the 'Residence Permit Fee' in student residence permit applications. Only the annually determined 'Residence Permit Card Fee' must be paid.
residencyPermitContent.faq4Question: What should I do if my address changes?
residencyPermitContent.faq4Answer: If your address or contact information changes, you are legally required to notify the Population Directorate first and then the Provincial Immigration Office within 20 business days. Otherwise, you may face penalties.
residencyPermitContent.ctaDescription: Our expert team is with you for university registration, equivalency, and residence permit applications. Minimize risks.

workPermitContent.title: Student Work Permit
workPermitContent.description: Working rights, application procedures, and critical legal details for foreign students studying in Turkey.
workPermitContent.legalRightsParagraph1: Conditions for foreign students' work permits are regulated by {law}. Work rights and limits are determined according to the level of education.
workPermitContent.associateBachelorDesc: Applications can be made after the {firstYear} and {partTime} work rights are granted.
workPermitContent.masterDoctorateDesc: Applications can be made from the beginning of education and {noLimit} does not apply.
workPermitContent.importantNoteDesc: Student residence permit alone does not grant work rights. Every student wishing to work must apply for a Work Permit through an official employer.
workPermitContent.minWageEmploymentDesc: Employers may not need to meet foreign employment quotas (e.g., 5 Turkish citizens per foreigner) in some exceptional cases, but professional application reduces rejection risk.
workPermitContent.studentEndDesc: If your student status ends for any reason (graduation, registration freeze, deletion, etc.), your student residence permit will be canceled, affecting the legal status of your work permit.
workPermitContent.howToApplyStep2: An agreement must be made with an employer, and the company official who is an E-Declaration user must upload to the Ministry of Labor and Social Security system.
workPermitContent.howToApplyStep3: Avoid rejections in this process with high refusal rates by getting support from Atasa Education experts to complete your application flawlessly.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion1: Why can't I apply in the first year?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer1: By law, associate and undergraduate students are not granted legal work rights in their first year to focus on adaptation and education. However, there is no such restriction for Master's and Doctorate students.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion2: Will my residence permit change if I get a work permit?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer2: No, a valid work permit also serves as a residence permit. However, since you are a student, it is important to maintain your rights related to school processes. Care should be taken when obtaining a work permit outside of education.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion3: Can my employer cancel my permit without my knowledge?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer3: Your employer is obliged to notify the Ministry within 15 days if you leave the job. After notification, your permit is canceled. If you want to work again, a new application must be made by your new employer.
workPermitContent.ctaDesc: Contact Atasa Consultants to avoid rejection in official procedures between your employer and the Ministry.

applySteps.title: Become a University Student in 8 Easy Steps!
applySteps.description: University applications are now very easy with Atasa Education. Our consultants will be with you every step of the way.
applySteps.step01Desc: To apply, you must first complete the Application Form completely. Upload the required documents through the relevant form. If you haven't decided which university to apply to yet, selecting the departments you are interested in is sufficient.
applySteps.step02Desc: When your application reaches us, our consultants will contact you via WhatsApp, email, or phone to provide information about your application and the process.
applySteps.step03Desc: You can visit our office for more detailed information and benefit from free consultancy services offered by our education experts. Our consultants will assist you on many topics, from university and department selection to quota status and payment terms.
applySteps.step04Desc: Based on your preferences and criteria, acceptance letters from our partner universities will be sent to you via email. These acceptance letters are not binding, so you are not obligated to enroll. You can consider these letters as offers.
applySteps.step05Desc: If you find the accepted university and department suitable, you need to make the tuition payment to the official bank accounts of the university specified in the offer letter. You can pay the tuition fee for the entire year at once or as a deposit at the beginning.
applySteps.step06Desc: After making the payment to the university, do not forget to send your payment receipt to your consultant via WhatsApp or email. Your consultant will share the receipt with the university to speed up the process.
applySteps.step07Desc: When your tuition payment reaches the university's bank account, the university will send your official acceptance letter to your email address.
applySteps.step08Desc: Prepare your official acceptance letter and all necessary documents, visit your university, and complete your registration.

serviceList.description: We manage all official procedures in Turkey under one roof with a professional and reliable approach.

serviceList.studentDesc: We provide support at every stage of the education process for students coming to Turkey from abroad for education, including university registrations, student visas, and equivalency procedures.
serviceList.consultancyDesc: We offer one-on-one support with academic consultancy, career planning, cultural adaptation programs, and more, tailored to the needs of each student and foreign citizen.

applyAdvantages.titleHighlight: Privileges of Applying with Atasa Education
applyAdvantages.description: We are here to provide you with the best support on your education journey in Turkey. Here are some of the advantages we offer:
applyAdvantages.advantage1: Applications are free. We are here to guide you. We look forward to helping you achieve your educational goals.
applyAdvantages.advantage2: You automatically benefit from exclusive discounts at Atasa Education. Also, you have access to special scholarship opportunities offered only through us.
applyAdvantages.advantage3: You can apply to multiple universities simultaneously with a single application form. Your acceptance letters will be sent directly to your email by the universities.
applyAdvantages.advantage4: You can benefit from special quotas, receive visa support if needed, and we also assist with dormitory or accommodation.
applyAdvantages.advantage5: Our experienced consultants will help you make the right department and university choices.
applyAdvantages.advantage6: We support you at every stage until you start university.
applyAdvantages.advantage7: Apply with the official representative of the university!
applyAdvantages.advantage8: We offer admission to universities without exams and only with your diploma grade.

cookiePolicyPage.pageDescription: Detailed information about cookies used on our website, their purposes, and how you can manage them.
cookiePolicyPage.headerDescription: Detailed information about cookies used on our website, their purposes, and how you can manage them.
cookiePolicyPage.introText: This disclosure text has been prepared to inform you about which personal data of yours is processed by the data controller; for what purpose, how, and why it is processed; with whom it is shared; and how long it is stored. The information is provided pursuant to Article 10 of the Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698.
cookiePolicyPage.companyDescription: "ATASA" or "Company" as the data controller, uses cookies to provide you with a better user experience when you visit our website, to improve our services, and to make our education and consultancy activities more effective in the digital environment.
cookiePolicyPage.lawReference: The use of these cookies is carried out in accordance with the relevant legislation we are subject to, primarily the Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 ("KVKK").
cookiePolicyPage.cookiePolicyPurpose: This Cookie Disclosure Text has been prepared to inform you about the purposes, types, and management of personal data collected through cookies placed on your device via our website.
cookiePolicyPage.cookieTypesDescription: The following types of cookies are used on our website:
cookiePolicyPage.sessionCookiesDesc: Deleted when you close your browser. They ensure the session is maintained during your visit.
cookiePolicyPage.persistentCookiesDesc: Remain on your device for a certain period. They perform functions such as remembering site preferences.
cookiePolicyPage.firstPartyCookiesDesc: Cookies placed directly by our site.
cookiePolicyPage.thirdPartyCookiesDesc: Cookies placed by our partners or service providers.
cookiePolicyPage.mandatoryCookiesDesc: Necessary for the basic functions of our website to work. For example; form filling, login processes.
cookiePolicyPage.performanceCookiesDesc: Analyze site traffic and determine the most visited pages. This data is anonymous.
cookiePolicyPage.advertisingCookiesDesc: Used to provide content and consultancy services tailored to your interests.
cookiePolicyPage.functionalCookiesDesc: Used to remember your preferences on the website (language, country selection, user preferences).
cookiePolicyPage.processingPurposesTitle: Purposes and Legal Reasons for Processing Personal Data
cookiePolicyPage.mandatoryProcessingDesc: Mandatory cookies: processed necessarily for the provision of the service directly related to the establishment or performance of a contract (e.g., online consultancy application, contact forms) pursuant to Article 5/2(c) of KVKK.
cookiePolicyPage.analyticsProcessingDesc: Analytical and performance cookies: used based on your explicit consent pursuant to Article 5/1 of KVKK.
cookiePolicyPage.advertisingProcessingDesc: Advertising/Marketing cookies: processed based on your explicit consent to provide content tailored to your interests.
cookiePolicyPage.functionalProcessingDesc: Functional cookies: also used with your explicit consent to remember preferences.
cookiePolicyPage.collectionMethodDesc: Your personal data is collected automatically in digital environments through cookies used during your visit to our website.
cookiePolicyPage.preferencesManagementDesc: Through the 'Preferences' or 'Cookie Settings' link at the bottom of our website, you can set and change your preferences for all cookie types except Mandatory Cookies at any time.
cookiePolicyPage.personalDataTransferDesc: Your personal data may be shared with service providers, partners, and authorized institutions within the country, limited to the purposes stated above and in accordance with applicable legislation.
cookiePolicyPage.rightsIntro: Under Article 11 of KVKK, regarding your personal data processed through cookies, you have the rights to:
cookiePolicyPage.rightCorrectionDeletion: Request correction, deletion, or destruction if processed,
cookiePolicyPage.contactInfoDesc: You can submit your requests in writing or electronically signed to support@atasa.tr in accordance with the Procedures and Principles for Application to the Data Controller.
cookiePolicyPage.dataControllerAddress: Mecidiyeköy Mah. Raşit Rıza St. Ahmet Esin Business Center NO:4 Floor:2 Apt:7, Şişli/Istanbul

kvkkPage.pageDescription: Your personal data security is our priority. Detailed information on how your data is processed.
kvkkPage.headerDescription: Your personal data security is our priority. Below you can find detailed information on how your data is processed.
kvkkPage.mainHeading: DISCLOSURE AND COMMUNICATION CONSENT TEXT UNDER THE PERSONAL DATA PROTECTION LAW
kvkkPage.introText: This disclosure text has been prepared to inform you about which personal data of yours is processed by the data controller; for what purpose, how, and why it is processed; with whom it is shared; and how long it is stored. The information is provided pursuant to Article 10 of the Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698.
kvkkPage.section1Content: Pursuant to the Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 ("KVKK"), your personal data is processed by the data controller
kvkkPage.section1Address: (Address: Mecidiyeköy Mah. Raşit Rıza St. Ahmet Esin Business Center NO:4 Floor:2 Apt:7, Şişli / Istanbul).
kvkkPage.section2Intro: Your collected personal data will be processed for the purposes of:
kvkkPage.purpose2: Making information, campaign, and school announcements
kvkkPage.purpose5: Managing your university application and acceptance processes,
kvkkPage.section2Note: in accordance with Articles 5 and 6 of KVKK.
kvkkPage.section3Content: Name, surname, phone number, email address, education background information, passport/ID details, request/message contents, and other academic documents/information shared during the university application process.
kvkkPage.section4Content: Your personal data may be transferred to partner universities, educational institutions, visa consultants, IT infrastructure providers, and authorized legal institutions in accordance with Articles 8 and 9 of KVKK.
kvkkPage.section5Title: Methods and Legal Reasons for Collecting Personal Data
kvkkPage.section5Content: Your personal data is collected through our office, website (application and contact forms), email, phone, social media, and seminar/fair events based on the legal grounds of service provision, contract establishment, legal obligation, and explicit consent.
kvkkPage.rights4: Learn the third parties to whom it is transferred
kvkkPage.contactDescription: You can submit your applications and requests in writing to our head office or via our email address.
kvkkPage.officeAddress: Mecidiyeköy Mah. Raşit Rıza St. Ahmet Esin Business Center NO:4 Floor:2 Apt:7, Şişli / Istanbul
